name: Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo
name_original: Subida a la Peña de Francia — circular desde El Maíllo
description: This difficult 30.0-mile (48.2 km) road cycling loop from El Maíllo climbs Peña de Francia, offering panoramic views and a significant chall
region_intro: Road cycling around El Maíllo offers routes through the Sierra de Francia comarca, a region characterized by its diverse ecosystems within the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park. The terrain features extensive forests of oak, pine, and holm oak, alongside valleys and challenging ascents, including the prominent Peña de Francia. Winding roads provide varied elevations and scenic views across this protected natural area. Best road cycling routes around El Maíllo The most popular road cycling route is Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo, a 29.97 miles (48.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes a significant ascent to Peña de Francia. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto del Portillo and Puerto de Robledo Loop, a difficult 59.16 miles (95.2 km) path. This route features extensive climbing through varied mountain landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the La Alberca Main Square – Portillo Viewpoint loop from El Maíllo, a 47.99 miles (77.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Francia, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around El Maíllo is defined by challenging mountain roads, serene valleys, and protected natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in El Maíllo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Maíllo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Maíllo?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around El Maíllo, offering a wide range of options for exploring the Sierra de Francia comarca. These routes traverse diverse ecosystems within the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, providing varied terrain from challenging ascents to serene valleys.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for road cycling routes in El Maíllo?
A: Road cycling routes in El Maíllo are generally considered moderate to difficult. Approximately 16 routes are rated moderate, while 14 are classified as difficult. This reflects the region's mountainous landscape, featuring significant climbs and varied elevations, making it ideal for cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while cycling in the region?
A: The El Maíllo area, part of the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, offers stunning natural features. You can cycle towards the prominent Peña de Francia, a peak offering panoramic views. Other highlights include the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1560136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Lobos Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around El Maíllo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around El Maíllo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025238282/pena-de-francia-cima-loop-from-el-maillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025739729/alto-del-portillo-and-puerto-de-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Portillo and Puerto de Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Maíllo?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for road cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can also be suitable, especially for routes that offer shade from the extensive oak and pine forests, though some ascents might be more demanding in higher temperatures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in El Maíllo?
A: While many routes in El Maíllo feature challenging ascents, the region does offer varied terrain. For families, it's advisable to look for routes with less elevation gain or consider sections of longer routes that are flatter. The Natural Park also features accessible routes like 'Los Molinos' which could be adapted for family outings, though specific road cycling routes designed purely for beginners or young children are less common due to the mountainous nature of the area.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in El Maíllo?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in El Maíllo, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain roads, the serene valleys, and the stunning views across the protected natural park landscapes. The diverse ecosystems and the rewarding climbs, such as to Peña de Francia, are frequently highlighted.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes, pubs, or accommodation suitable for cyclists in the El Maíllo area?
A: El Maíllo and nearby villages like La Alberca, Miranda del Castañar, Cepeda, and San Martín del Castañar offer various amenities. La Alberca, in particular, is known for its charming atmosphere and provides options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation that cater to visitors, including cyclists. These villages serve as excellent bases for exploring the surrounding road cycling routes.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in El Maíllo or nearby towns?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in El Maíllo and the surrounding towns such as La Alberca and Miranda del Castañar. These villages serve as common starting points for routes, and you can usually find public parking areas suitable for leaving your vehicle while you cycle.

Q: Are permits required for cycling within the Sierra de Francia y Las Batuecas Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Francia y Las Batuecas Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and be mindful of protected areas. Stick to marked roads and trails, and avoid disturbing wildlife or natural habitats.

Q: Can I reach El Maíllo and the cycling routes using public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to El Maíllo and surrounding villages like La Alberca, Miranda del Castañar, and Cepeda may be limited, especially when traveling with a bicycle. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport in advance, as space for bikes can often be restricted or require prior booking.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer significant climbs?
A: Absolutely. The El Maíllo region is renowned for its challenging ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025238282/pena-de-francia-cima-loop-from-el-maillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo&lt;/a&gt;, with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025739729/alto-del-portillo-and-puerto-de-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Portillo and Puerto de Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, featuring nearly 2000 meters of climbing, are perfect for cyclists seeking demanding mountain challenges.
tips:
Ascent to the mythical Peña de Francia
Altitude: 1732m
Distance: 12km
Height difference: 720m
Average Slope: 6%

Not only does it give its name to the wonderful Natural Park, but its peak at 1,727 meters above sea level offers the best panoramic views in all directions. Right there, at the top, is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Peña de Francia of the Dominican Order that is worth visiting. And of course, you cannot return without having taken the obligatory photos at the viewpoint and the Balcony of Santiago, two panoramic points par excellence.
You can get here using the means that you like best, either by car, bike or walking, the last modality will give you several alternatives of very picturesque trails from the town of La Alberca or from Camping Sierra de Francia.
Great viewpoint of the Agadon Valley, a mandatory stop on the way up to Peña de Francia or the descent, to take more photos and read interesting facts on the information panel.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult road cycling route. It demands very good fitness due to its substantial elevation gain of 1,090 meters over 48.2 kilometers. The climb to Peña de Francia itself is sustained and steady, making it a challenging ascent.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on well-paved roads, making it suitable for race bikes. While some routes in the broader region might have unpaved segments, this specific loop from El Maíllo is mostly on good road surfaces, characterized by sustained ascents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Peña de Francia CIMA Loop?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the challenging Ascent to Peña de Francia, which is a recognized CIMA climb. At the summit, you'll find the historic Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia and enjoy 360-degree panoramic views. The route also passes through Los Lobos Pass (1,499 m).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation and potential for varying weather, the best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring, early summer, or early autumn months. These periods offer more stable weather conditions and pleasant temperatures for a challenging climb.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Peña de Francia CIMA Loop. The route is publicly accessible, and the area is part of the Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, which does not typically charge an entrance fee for general access.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the loop from El Maíllo?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas in El Maíllo are not detailed, as a starting point for a road cycling loop, you can typically find parking within the village itself. Look for public parking spaces or suitable roadside areas where parking is permitted and safe for your vehicle.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will encounter parts of the [CIMA SA06] Peña de Francia * Monsagro, [CIMA SA06] Peña de Francia * El Cabaco, and El Cabaco - Peña de Francia trails, among others, as you complete the loop.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers exceptional scenery as it winds through the beautiful Sierra de Francia, part of the Central System mountain range. You'll see picturesque landscapes with chestnut, oak, and pine trees, especially on the approach to the summit. The panoramic views from the top of Peña de Francia are particularly stunning.

faqs.question: Is the Peña de Francia CIMA Loop from El Maíllo suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is best suited for experienced road cyclists with very good fitness. The substantial elevation gain and sustained climbs make it unsuitable for beginners or family cycling outings looking for an easy ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this difficult road cycling route, you should bring appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a challenging road cycling route with significant climbs and descents, this loop is generally not suitable for dogs. The terrain and speed of cycling are not conducive to bringing pets along.
